title: IntentAnalysis
description: L2d — the ad's persuasion intent/strategy (axis 5) + the MEANING of its copy×layout bindings (axis 6). Ring 2, brand-free: it classifies what persuasion mechanism the ad USES, never whether that mechanism fits OUR brand/persona (that is the brief, ring 3). TEXT-ONLY: derived from copy-analysis + layout-analysis + visual-analysis + bindings, never the image. appeal/funnel_stage are the aggregatable enums; the rest is free-text rationale.

appeal.description: The dominant persuasion mechanism the ad uses (brand-free).

primary_objection_addressed.description: Free-text: the buyer objection this ad most works to neutralize (NOT aggregated).

binding_reading.description: Axis 6 MEANING — what each spatial text↔graphic binding (from the bindings artifact) DOES, e.g. a price tag placed ON the product = value-anchored-to-product framing. References bound_pairs by id.

look_copy_tension.description: If visual-analyst flagged a look↔copy mismatch (e.g. clean look wearing a raw social-proof device), the STRATEGY that mismatch encodes — read here, never re-labeled as a register.

evidence.description: Anti-hallucination trace: which analysis facts (copy hook/role, layout, visual register, binding) the appeal was derived from. An appeal with no basis is a defect.
